steps.title: Identify the cost-benefit equation
steps.content: The scientist concludes BZMA has “greatest potential” when “practical considerations”—meaning cost—are weighed in. This means BZMA must offer the best value: nearly matching the expensive β-cyclodextrin resin's absorption while far exceeding the other cheap resins.

steps.title: Find evidence of that trade-off
steps.content: Choice A confirms this: BZMA's absorption is only modestly lower than β-cyclodextrin (worth the cost difference) yet clearly higher than other cheap methacrylates (making it the best-value option). The other choices either show BZMA as non-unique among cheap resins (D), nearly equal to the expensive resin (C, which erases the cost advantage), or inconsistently superior across test compounds (B). That matches choice A — A is correct.
